mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in over 55% of instances when the unemployment threshold is breached, invalidating the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion that drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 60% of triggered cases or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the industrial production index during late-cycle expansions signal supply chain bottlenecks and profit margin compression that cause immediate negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative returns during late-cycle phases or produces positive returns in over 50% of contraction instances.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation exceeding 2.5% forces the Federal Reserve to accelerate monetary tightening, raising discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns within one month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 65% of high-inflation threshold breaches or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling Rank IC drops below 0.01 over a 24-month window or payroll growth shows no correlation with quarterly equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP growth and falling retail sales indicates inventory accumulation and weakening consumer demand that triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive returns in over 55% of cases or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production expansion coinciding with stable inflation during early-cycle recoveries signals robust capacity utilization that drives immediate positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative returns in over 60% of early-cycle instances or fails to outperform a baseline momentum strategy in out-of-sample testing.
